title: PhyMSNet: A Physics-Informed Multi-Scale Network for Robust and Interpretable Diagnosis of Bearings Under Variable Conditions
authors: Guanze Li, Hongqi Lin, Zhuyun Chen, Junyu Qi, Weihua Li, Qiang Liu
abstract: In engineering practice, rolling element bearings operate under varying conditions, generating nonstationary vibration signals with complex physical characteristics. Although deep learning methods using multi-head self-attention and multi-scale convolution improve feature representation, they often increase computational burden and offer limited physical interpretability, constraining diagnostic reliability in applications. To tackle this issue, PhyMSNet, a robust and interpretable physics-informed multi-scale network, is proposed. The core of the framework is the integration of physical priors with a multi-scale learning architecture. First, raw vibration signals are processed through cyclic spectrum analysis to extract modulation features intrinsically related to fault characteristics. These physically grounded features are then fed into a multi-scale convolutional network designed to capture fault signatures across different temporal resolutions. To further align model focus with underlying failure mechanisms, a Physics-informed Squeeze-and-Excitation Module (PhySE) is embedded within the network. This module leverages prior knowledge of system resonance and transient impulse patterns to adaptively recalibrate feature importance. Moreover, for enhanced transparency, the model decision logic is quantitatively explained using Shapley additive explanations (SHAP), identifying contributions of spectral and temporal features to the final diagnosis. Comprehensive experiments on a CNC fault dataset with varying speeds and loads demonstrate that PhyMSNet achieves superior diagnostic accuracy, stronger robustness to condition changes, and clearer interpretability compared to state-of-the-art deep learning methods.

title: A Bidirectional Shunt-Switch D-Band Mixer with a Double-Balanced Combining-Filter in a 130-nm BiCMOS Technology
authors: Alexander Tiefenbach, Lars Zimmermann, Dietmar Kissinger
abstract: This paper introduces a double-balanced shunt-switch mixer in a 130-nm SiGe technology. The proposed mixer is capable of bidirectional signal conversion between a 60 GHz baseband and the full D-band. The mixer has a conversion gain of -12.9 dB in the down-conversion direction and -11.9 dB in the up-conversion direction. Linearity is characterized by an input-referred 1-dB compression point of 8 dBm. Utilizing a double-balanced architecture realized by four shunt-switch mixing cores seperated by mixed-mode low- and high-pass filters, the design achieves LO-RF and BB-RF isolation levels exceeding 40 dB. The proposed mixer operates with a DC power consumption of 15.3 mW and a LO power of 4 dBm. The core area of the mixer is 320 µm x 160 µm, while the total chip, including a LO mulitplier and buffer, as well as baluns for measurement, occupies an area of 0.65 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: ShipGen: Improving Remote Sensing Ship Detection Via Layout-Conditioned Diffusion-Based Data Augmentation
authors: Yuhan Zhang, Jun Liao, Jie Zhou, Yuanpei Chen, Xiaode Liu, Weihang Peng, Hongyu Yang, Qi Ming, Yufei Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) ship detection plays a key role in the field of remote sensing. Data augmentation is frequently used to enhance detector performance due to the common challenge of limited annotated data. However, most augmented methods are limited to geometric transformations, “copy-paste”, or image inpainting, which are guided by strict constraints and suffer limited target and background diversity. To address this, we propose a novel multi-phase transfer-learned framework with layout-conditioned diffusion model. It incorporates sparse target layouts as relaxed conditions into the latent diffusion model, where the conditions take the form of bounding boxes that specify target positions and sizes. This leaves other ship attributes (e.g. orientation, scattering characteristics) and backgrounds to be freely generated and enhance target and background diversity. For enhancing the model's convergence on small datasets, we first pre-train it on large-scale datasets and then fine-tune it on small-scale ones for transfer learning. This enables precise layout-guided target generation even when using small datasets. For subsequent data augmentation, we adopt the widely used grounded-language-to-image generation (GLIGEN) model for practical use, and based on the transfer-learned model, we further develop a new automatic augmentation pipeline with a filtering mechanism. This pipeline integrates multiple foundation models and tools, including layout augmentation, segment anything model (SAM)-based annotation correction, and data filtering, and can automatically adjust inappropriate annotations and screen high-quality data to serve as augmented data for downstream detection tasks. Experiments on SSDD and HRSID datasets validate that the pipeline improves multiple detectors' performance across in-shore and off-shore scenarios, and qualitative analyses confirm high diversity and quality of generated images.

title: EfficientEndoSeg: A Lightweight YOLOv5-Based Framework for Endoscopic Surgical Instrument Segmentation and Skill-Related Motion Analysis
authors: Thai Dinh Kim, Quang-Anh Nguyen-Duc, Minh-Duc Pham, Duy-Tung Doan, Manh-Hung Nguyen, Xuan-Hai Le, Ngoc-Thanh Pham
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of achieving accurate and real-time surgical instrument instance segmentation under strict computational constraints in minimally invasive surgery by proposing EfficientEndoSeg, a compact and high-throughput segmentation framework coupled with tool-tip analysis. Building on the You Only Look Once (YOLO) version 5 instance segmentation baseline, EfficientEndoSeg incorporates four targeted architectural refinements: the C3SG2 module combining GhostNetV2 and Swin Transformer components to enhance local–global representation, Ghost Convolution for parameter-efficient feature extraction, a Double-Attention (A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) block for contextual refinement, and Complete Intersection-over-Union–guided Soft Non-Maximum Suppression to improve robustness in crowded surgical scenes. The framework is systematically evaluated on an internally annotated extension of the m2cai16-tool dataset, comprising 5,249 endoscopic frames and 8,785 polygon-level annotations across seven instrument categories, which serves as a controlled benchmark for experimental validation. These architectural modifications reduce model parameters by 25% and increase inference throughput by 52.7%, while consistently improving recall and mean Average Precision across multiple Intersection-over-Union thresholds. The proposed model matches or surpasses recent YOLO version 7 to version 12 variants and remains competitive with transformer-based and two-stage frameworks, including SegFormer, ISINet, LETNet, Detectron2, and Mask R-CNN. In addition, a training-free tool-tip tracking module based on modified K3M skeletonization enables trajectory, heatmap, and usage analyses, facilitating qualitative skill-related motion assessment without additional learning overhead. Overall, EfficientEndoSeg delivers a reproducible, lightweight, and deployment-ready solution for segmentation-driven surgical scene understanding and instrument motion analysis.

title: FusionRank: An Ensemble Approach for Unsupervised Extractive Summarization Using Probabilistic and Sentence Embedding-Based Features
authors: Maya ALGhafri, Imran Khan, Abdelhamid Abdessalem
abstract: Text summarization empowers users with meaningful summaries that help them quickly grasp essential insights while reducing cognitive load. Despite its importance, text summarization encounters numerous obstacles that limit its efficacy. Key obstacles include balancing accuracy with computational efficiency, especially when handling large-scale unstructured datasets. Furthermore, generating concise and contextually relevant summaries remains a significant challenge. In this study, we introduce a novel unsupervised rank fusion framework for single-document summarization, paving the way for more reliable and scalable summarization solutions. This approach integrates three sentence-level features. In the first phase, topic relevancy is derived from probabilistic topic modeling using Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA). In the second phase, semantic content is derived from sentence embeddings based on large language models (LLMs). In the third phase, the sentence importance is calculated using a Markov chain model. The final significance score of each sentence is calculated through a weighted fusion driven by individual feature-based rankings. The performance of the proposed framework is validated through experiments on the widely used CNN/DailyMail and WikiHow datasets. On the CNN/DailyMail dataset, the developed approach achieves ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L scores of 44.9, 24.9, and 43.6, respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art unsupervised methods. On WikiHow, it attains 35.91, 14.9, and 34.2. The method demonstrates superior performance, consistently outperforming current state-of-the-art summarization techniques by generating summaries that are coherent, informative, and concise. The summaries generated can be effectively applied in real-world scenarios, such as academic literature review platforms, news aggregation systems, and decision support applications.

title: Compressed-Sensing-Based Virtual Array Reconstruction for Multi-Node OFDM Radar
authors: Áron Szabó, Jiayi Chen, Zsolt Kollár, Benjamin Nuss, Thomas Zwick
abstract: This paper presents a technique for generating a wide-aperture virtual uniform linear array (ULA) using cooperative orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) multiple input multiple output (MIMO) radar nodes. A generalized phase-reconstruction approach enabled consistent alignment of bistatic and monostatic measurements, while compressed sensing completed the sparse array to enable high-resolution direction of arrival (DoA) estimation. Simulation studies confirmed notable performance gains, including reduced sidelobe levels and a 50% lower root-mean-square localization error compared with individual node processing. The proposed architecture shows promise for scalable and computationally tractable implementation, suggesting a viable path toward real-time, high-accuracy sensing in distributed automotive radar networks.

title: Cross-Modal Feature Enhancement-Based Few-Shot Object Detection Method for Transmission Line Inspection
authors: Cui Zanchen, Tong Ying, Shao Yuwen, Wu Kang, Wang Zhe, Yang Dewei, Han Jueqing
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: This study addresses the common issue of sample imbalance (Small-Shot Problem) in power transmission line object detection, specifically the challenge of insufficient feature learning for critical rare components (such as vibration dampers and bolts). We propose a Cross-Modal Feature Enhanced Few-Shot Object Detection method for power transmission lines. The method uses the powerful large-scale Vision-Language Model (VLM), Florence-2, as its foundational framework. The core innovation is the introduction of a Feature Enhancement Module (FEM), which aims to supplement and enhance the visual features of rare samples through a cross-modal knowledge injection strategy. This strategy leverages the rich text semantic information from the pretrained model CLIP. The FEM generates a knowledge vector by calculating the cosine similarity between the rare category's CLIP text features and the rich sample's CLIP image features for weighted summation. This vector is then efficiently fused into the visual features during model training. Experimental validation was conducted on a self-built power transmission line inspection dataset. The results show that the proposed method achieved a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 0.7480 in overall performance, comprehensively outperforming mainstream models such as Faster R-CNN and Mask R-CNN. More importantly, this strategy successfully solved the sample imbalance problem, leading to a significant improvement in both the F1 score and AP value for the rarest category, vibration damper, among the three target classes (insulator, bolt, vibration damper). It also produced a positive synergistic effect on other rare categories like bolts, demonstrating its high robustness and practical value in real-world power transmission line inspection applications.

title: Security Knowledge in Books
authors: Paul C. van Oorschot
abstract: Students today largely avoid books. Free videos are easier; artificial intelligence tools are superficially faster. As a result, security books by true experts, some with unparalleled clarity and insight, remain unread. We offer a nod to books as ongoing sources of security knowledge.

title: Multi-Objective ISAC for Low-Altitude Economy Based on Multi-Task Deep Reinforcement Learning With Mixture of Experts
authors: Xiaowen Ye, Hengyi Lin, Xianxin Song, Yi Wu, Liqun Fu
abstract: This paper studies a low-altitude economy (LAE)-oriented terrestrial integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where a ground base station (GBS) provides communication and navigation services for authorized unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), as well as senses the low-altitude airspace to monitor the unauthorized mobile target. Both the communication sum-rate and sensing signal-to-noise ratio are maximized through joint optimization of GBS's beamforming and authorized UAVs' trajectories, subject to constraints including UAV mission completion and collision avoidance, as well as the GBS's maximum transmit power. To address this problem, we propose an intelligent joint optimization scheme, termed Multi-task mixture Of Drl Experts (MODE), based on multi-task deep reinforcement learning (DRL) with mixture of experts (MoE). Specifically, the MODE scheme first introduces the deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm to handle all continuous-domain variables and incorporates an objective-preference weight to capture the trade-off between communication and sensing performance. Thereafter, a multi-task MoE architecture is developed as the neural network of MODE, which treats each optimization problem with a specific objective-preference weight as a distinct task and trains these tasks concurrently within shared gating and expert networks. As such, MODE can generalize well to unseen weights. To facilitate efficient learning of the multi-task MoE architecture, we further put forth a hybrid experience replay mechanism that utilizes multi-task experience sets for training; each set contains all experiences from a complete episode under a specific task. Simulation results show that MODE attains more Pareto-efficient solutions than other schemes while maintaining stronger robustness across various simulation setups. Besides, we demonstrate the generalization ability of MODE for unseen tasks.

title: Selective Hybrid Processing Method of Sample Images Using Adaptive Regional Partitioning
authors: Ying Liu, Bingyang Xu, Dongxu Cheng, Suijun Liu, Xin Zhang, Binqiang Tian, Zhou Ou, Yaohe Li
abstract: During sample surface quality inspection, the acquired images often suffer from low spatial resolution and mixed noise due to uneven illumination, slight vibration of the inspection device, and the performance limitations of image acquisition equipment, thereby degrading subsequent defect identification and quality evaluation. To address this problem, this paper proposes a selective hybrid processing method for sample images based on adaptive regional partitioning. First, according to local structural characteristics and noise conditions, the input image is divided into noise-free blocks, edge blocks, suspected noisy blocks, and noise blocks. Then, weighted root-mean-square filtering and Gaussian filtering are selectively assigned to different block types so as to suppress noise while preserving original information and edge details as much as possible. Subsequently, based on regional smoothness, the denoised image is further divided into smooth and non-smooth regions, for which different interpolation reconstruction strategies are employed. Specifically, for non-smooth regions, a root-mean-square nonlinear interpolation model is constructed by incorporating the properties of the Sigmoid function and local asymmetry to enhance the reconstruction of edges and texture details, whereas bilinear interpolation is adopted for smooth regions to reduce computational complexity and improve processing efficiency. Experimental results on both natural images and sample inspection images demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a favorable balance among visual quality, objective evaluation metrics, and computational efficiency, especially under mixed-noise conditions. These results indicate that the proposed method effectively improves sample image quality and inspection efficiency, providing a practical image processing approach for sample surface quality inspection under complex noise conditions.

title: Scalable and Efficient Continual Learning from Demonstration via a Hypernetwork-generated Stable Dynamics Model_supp2-3692632.pdf
authors: Sayantan Auddy

title: Low-Altitude ISAC with Rotatable Active and Passive Arrays
authors: Ziyuan Zheng, Qingqing Wu, Yanze Zhu, Honghao Wang, Ying Gao, Wen Chen, Jian Xiong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: This paper investigates a low-altitude integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system that leverages cooperatively rotatable active and passive arrays. We consider a downlink scenario in which a base station (BS) equipped with a three-dimensional (3D) rotatable active array serves multiple users, with the transmit beampattern designed for low-altitude sensing, and assisted by a rotatable, reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). To capture the impact of array orientations, we develop a rotation-aware, geometry-based multipath model in which 3D rotations jointly affect the steering-vector phases via rotated element coordinates and the direction-dependent element and antenna radiation gains via a practical directivity model. On this basis, we formulate a joint design problem that maximizes a weighted-sum utility of the downlink sum rate and the sensing beampattern fidelity, where the latter is quantified by a normalized mean squared error (NMSE). We introduce a scaling parameter and update it in closed form once per outer alternating-optimization (AO) iteration. The resulting non-convex problem is tackled via an efficient AO framework that alternately updates the BS precoder, RIS phase shifts, and array rotation angles, using a quadratic transform and majorization-minimization-based closed-form updates, Riemannian conjugate gradient on the unit-modulus manifold, and projected gradient ascent under rotation box constraints, respectively. Numerical results show that the proposed jointly rotatable arrays at the BS and RIS achieve a favorable rate-sensing trade-off and yield performance gains over fixed or partially rotatable baselines.

title: A Diffusion Model-based Suppression Method for Complex and Unseen SAR RFI
authors: Liuyang Tang, Jiyuan Liu, Tao Zhang, Jin Ma, Feiming Wei, Huilin Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In the increasingly complex electromagnetic environment, radio frequency interference (RFI) poses a significant challenge to synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems, degrading image quality and corrupting valuable data. Existing deep learning-based suppression methods, which typically learn a direct mapping from contaminated data to clean data, are limited to the interference types encountered during training, leading to poor generalization to unknown RFI patterns and novel scenes. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a diffusion model-based method through reformulating RFI suppression as a conditional generative task. Conditioned on the uncorrupted information, the proposed model executes generative restoration of contaminated data by learning the distribution of clean SAR signals in the time-frequency domain. This generative paradigm enables the model robust to complex and unseen RFI patterns, as it aims to learn the clean data distribution rather than to recognize specific RFI types. To further enhance generalization across diverse RFI scenarios, the model is trained using a library of physically simulated RFI masks. Besides, we also introduce a composite loss function that incorporates the low-rank property of SAR data to enforce structural consistency. Experiments tested on Sentinel-1 data confirm the method’s superiority in complex and unknown RFI suppression, especially for the geographical regions that were completely unseen during training.

title: Multivariate prediction model with adaptive kernel configuration based on asymmetric transfer entropy and fuzzy $C$-means in CNN-Transformer
authors: Haonan Hu, Jianming Zhan, Jin Hee Yoon, Weiping Ding
abstract: In the era of digital transformation, the large-scale deployment of sensors has led to the collection of highly complex and diverse data, posing significant challenges for multivariate time series forecasting (MTSF). Traditional forecasting approaches, often based on linear assumptions, are limited in their ability to capture the nonlinear temporal dynamics prevalent in real-world scenarios. To address these challenges, this study proposes an innovative multivariate prediction framework that integrates deep learning with traditional machine learning techniques. The framework incorporates an asymmetric transfer entropy coefficient (ATC) to identify genuine causal relationships among features, constructing a directed graph for feature importance ranking. This mechanism enhances feature selection by capturing both dynamic and static relationships among variables. An enhanced fuzzy C-means clustering algorithm, SCFCM, is introduced, which incorporates cosine similarity and Euclidean distance to improve sample discriminability in high-dimensional spaces and enhance clustering accuracy. Bayesian optimization is employed to dynamically determine the kernel sizes and numbers of the CNN-Transformer (Convolutional neural network-Transformer) prediction network, thereby improving feature extraction efficiency. The unified architecture integrates feature selection, clustering, and forecasting, achieving superior predictive performance. This comprehensive prediction model is referred to as ATC-SCFCM-DKCNT. Experiments on six real-world datasets demonstrate that ATC-SCFCM-DKCNT consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of prediction accuracy and computational efficiency, highlighting its strong generalization ability and robustness in handling complex, high-dimensional data.

title: A Learning-Based Estimation and Fault-Tolerant Control Scheme for Uncertain Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Jialu Liu, Jiuxiang Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The learning-based cooperative control problem is investigated for the heterogeneous multi-agent system affected by unknown system uncertainties, actuator faults, and external disturbances. A learning-based estimation scheme is proposed to simultaneously identify unknown uncertainties, actuator faults, and disturbances. Exponential convergence of estimation errors is guaranteed by the designed learning laws without requiring persistent excitation. Based on the time-varying system matrix estimation, a constrained matrix observer is developed to satisfy specified matrix inequalities, thereby guaranteeing the stability of the closed-loop system. Furthermore, a distributed cooperative controller is designed for virtual agents to achieve synchronization with the leader while preventing fault propagation. Subsequently, a fault-tolerant controller is constructed for the actual heterogeneous agents, ensuring convergence between the faulty agents and the leader. Theoretical results are validated through simulations of connected vehicle platoons incorporating realistic vehicle dynamics and fault scenarios. Finally, the superiority of the proposed method regarding transient performance and control efficiency is highlighted through comparisons with existing adaptive learning techniques.

title: Prompt-Guided Disentanglement and Fusion Framework for Cross-Domain Fault Diagnosis With Class Mismatch
authors: Zuoyi Chen, Jun Wu, Hong-Zhong Huang
abstract: Most existing cross-domain fault diagnosis methods rely on the assumption of class consistency between source and target domains. However, this assumption is frequently violated in practical industrial scenarios, where varying operating conditions often correspond to inconsistent fault type sets (i.e., class mismatch). To address this challenge, this article proposes a prompt-guided disentanglement and fusion (PGDF) framework, which leverages the inherent semantic structure of natural language to guide the disentanglement of machine states under different operating conditions. Specifically, PGDF constructs learnable prompts combined with domain and class tokens to construct structured joint representations. Furthermore, a cross-modal decomposed fusion module is designed to disentangle linguistic features into independent domain and class factors. Crucially, through a disentanglement-recombination strategy, these factors are fused with observational data, enabling the PGDF to learn expressive features that independently capture domain and class relationships. Extensive experiments on four datasets involving cross-domain, cross-unseen domain, and cross-machine scenarios demonstrate that PGDF significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in diagnostic performance.

title: Efficient NoC for Embedded Heterogeneous Multi-Core DSPs
authors: Wei Chen, Dake Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: At present, most networks on chips (NoCs) are designed for symmetric multiprocessing architectures for general-purpose computing. While currently designed NoCs provide considerable flexibility, they are accompanied by substantial routing delays and a relatively high cost associated with reorder buffer memory. The significant difference in data transmission length is a characteristic of heterogeneous multi-core systems. We propose an innovative NoC architecture for embedded heterogeneous multi-core digital signal processor (DSP) systems, which includes the design of top-level architecture, router, network interface (NI), and NoC pipeline. Our designed NoC architecture has the advantages of flexibility and low latency. It can not only significantly improve the transmission performance of long data packets but also improve the transmission efficiency of short data packets. Moreover, we propose efficient routing methods, which include an efficient routing algorithm, a hybrid transmission method, a packet-connected circuit transmission method, a short packet transmission method, and multicast and broadcast transmission methods. We also propose fault-tolerant data transmission mechanisms, which include a fault-tolerant data transmission algorithm and a deadlock avoidance method. Our proposed method and mechanism can improve the performance and reliability of NoC, mitigate the impact of router failures, eliminate the need for reorder buffer memory, reduce power consumption, and improve the robustness of NoC. The experiments show the performance advantages of our design in terms of latency, throughput, and silicon overhead in comparison with state-of-the-art NoCs. Our design reduced the latency, area, and power consumption by 47%, 92.6%, and 78.9%, respectively, and improved the throughput by 27%. This verifies its effectiveness in high-end applications.

title: Onboard Real-Time Hyperspectral Subpixel Mapping With Pushbroom Neural Network
authors: Matteo Impieri, Davide Piccinini, Diego Valsesia, Enrico Magli
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite hyperspectral images provide fine spectral information but often suffer from coarse spatial resolution. This may result in mixed pixels containing multiple land cover classes, degrading the performance of conventional classification methods. Subpixel mapping seeks to assign class labels to multiple subpixels within each coarse pixel, producing a super-resolved classification map. Existing approaches rely on priors that are either handcrafted or obtained via large deep learning architectures for offline processing requiring substantial memory and computational resources. In this work, we focus on the processing setting directly onboard the satellite, where such priors are not available and there are strict constraints on computational complexity. In particular, we propose a line-based deep subpixel mapping network tailored for real-time deployment on satellites with a processing strategy that aligns with the pushbroom acquisition of hyperspectral sensors, named Deep Pushbroom SubPixel Mapping (DPSPM). The model predicts a super-resolved classification map exploiting spatial–spectral context from neighboring lines, along with long-range information from previous lines through efficient state-space models. The method achieves fast inference and has low memory requirements compatible with resource-constrained satellite platforms. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art subpixel hyperspectral mapping accuracy, while supporting real-time inference at the line acquisition rate, as tested on a low-power accelerator. Code and pretrained models will be released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/DavidePiccinini98/DeepPushbroomSubPixelMapping</uri>.

title: Signal-Geometry Optimization for Micro-Expression Recognition: A Dynamic Gradient Gain Perspective
authors: Qi Zeng, Rujun Xu, Cong Li
abstract: Micro-expression recognition (MER) poses a fundamental signal processing challenge: extracting transient, low-amplitude facial dynamics from video data dominated by high-energy stationary noise. Standard Cross-Entropy optimization often fails to enforce the intra-class compactness required to resolve these low-Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) patterns, yielding embedding manifolds with poor discriminability. To address this, we propose SGO-Net, a Signal-Geometry Optimization network. Diverging from Euclidean paradigms, we enforce a hyperspherical embedding constraint to explicitly decouple signal direction from amplitude-dependent noise. From a dynamic gradient gain perspective, we theoretically reformulate the Hyperspherical Proxy Anchor Loss, proving that it guarantees a gradient magnitude strictly monotonic to the angular deviation. This mechanism acts as a signal-adaptive attentional gain that amplifies supervisory signals for hard, low-SNR samples. Extensive experiments on the composite benchmark (CASME II, SAMM, SMIC) demonstrate a state-of-the-art Unweighted F1-score of 0.896. Notably, perturbation analysis validates that this geometric-dynamic synergy offers superior stability over static angular margin baselines in reliable signal recovery.

title: Integrating Brain-Computer Interface and Neuromorphic Computing for Human Digital Twins
authors: Chen Shang, Jiadong Yu, Dinh Thai Hoang
abstract: The integration of immersive communication into a human-centric ecosystem has intensified the demand for sophisticated Human Digital Twins (HDTs) driven by multifaceted human data. However, the effective construction of HDTs faces significant challenges due to the heterogeneity of data collection devices, the high energy demands associated with processing intricate data, and concerns over the privacy of sensitive information. This work introduces a novel biologically-inspired (bio-inspired) HDT framework that leverages Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) sensor technology to capture brain signals as the data source for constructing HDTs. By collecting and analyzing these signals, the framework not only minimizes device heterogeneity and enhances data collection efficiency, but also provides richer and more nuanced physiological and psychological data for constructing personalized HDTs. To this end, we further propose a bio-inspired neuromorphic computing learning model based on the Spiking Neural Network (SNN). This model utilizes discrete neural spikes to emulate the way the human brain processes information, thereby enhancing the system's ability to process data effectively while reducing energy consumption. Additionally, we integrate a Federated Learning (FL) strategy within the model to strengthen data privacy. We then conduct a case study to demonstrate the performance of our proposed twofold bio-inspired scheme. Finally, we present several challenges and promising directions for future research on HDTs driven by bio-inspired technologies.

title: An Aeromagnetic Compensation Model for Vibration-induced Magnetic Interference without Additional Sensors
authors: Changping Du, Haidong Wang, Hong Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Aeromagnetic compensation is a critical process in airborne magnetic anomaly detection (MAD), aimed at reducing magnetic interference to improve the detection signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). In practice, detection magnetometers are typically mounted at the end of an external boom to minimize interference from the aircraft. However, the non-rigid connection between the boom and fuselage inevitably induces relative vibration, which introduces both geomagnetic gradient interference and aircraft magnetic gradient interference. To address this issue, this paper proposes a compensation method for vibration-induced magnetic interference using only a fluxgate magnetometer and an attitude sensor. A set of nonlinear equations is first established through direction cosine matrices, which link the attitude sensor readings, the relative vibration angles between the boom and fuselage, and the fluxgate magnetometer measurements. The Levenberg-Marquardt (L-M) optimization algorithm is then employed to solve for the relative vibration angles. Subsequently, a vibration-induced magnetic interference compensation model is constructed by integrating the estimated angles and boom length with the aircraft magnetic gradient model and the geomagnetic gradient model. Finally, combined with the traditional Tolles-Lawson (T-L) model, an integrated aeromagnetic compensation model is constructed, which can more effectively reduce platform-generated magnetic interference. Field experiments demonstrate that this method suppressed the vibration-induced interference to improve the compensation accuracy. Compared with the T-L model, the compensation model proposed in this paper achieves an increase of 3 to 4 in the Improvement Ratio (IR) of the standard deviation (STD) of the compensated data, and an equivalent increase of 3 to 4 in the IR of the Figure of Merit (FOM).

title: A Study of the Removability of Speaker-Adversarial Perturbations
authors: Liping Chen, Chenyang Guo, Kong Aik Lee, Zhen-Hua Ling, Wu Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Recent advancements in adversarial attacks have demonstrated their effectiveness in misleading speaker recognition models, making wrong predictions about speaker identities. On the other hand, defense techniques against speaker-adversarial attacks focus on reducing the effects of speaker-adversarial perturbations on speaker attribute extraction. These techniques do not seek to fully remove the perturbations and restore the original speech. To this end, this paper studies the removability of speaker-adversarial perturbations. Specifically, the investigation is conducted assuming various degrees of awareness of the perturbation generator across three scenarios: ignorant, semi-informed, and well-informed. Besides, we consider both the optimization-based and feedforward perturbation generation methods. Experiments conducted on the LibriSpeech dataset demonstrated that: 1) in the ignorant scenario, speaker-adversarial perturbations cannot be eliminated, although their impact on speaker attribute extraction is reduced, 2) in the semi-informed scenario, the speaker-adversarial perturbations cannot be fully removed, while those generated by the feedforward model can be considerably reduced, and 3) in the well-informed scenario, speaker-adversarial perturbations are nearly eliminated, allowing for the restoration of the original speech.

title: Plasma Force Diagnostics Based on Acoustic Signal Measurement
authors: Zhiqiang Wang, Bohan Ye, Chuncheng Chen, Yinke Dou, Lanlan Nie, Xinpei Lu
abstract: The physical characteristics of nonthermal atmospheric-pressure plasma jets (N-APPJs) have been extensively investigated, whereas their associated acoustic signatures remain insufficiently understood. In this work, we measure the acoustic signals generated by N-APPJs under various electrical and gas conditions using a calibrated acoustic detector. Fourier transform analysis reveals that the acoustic spectra consist of harmonic components whose frequencies correspond to integer multiples of the applied high-voltage driving frequency. Among these components, the second harmonic exhibits the highest amplitude. Notably, the amplitude of the second harmonic shows a strong correlation with the mechanical force exerted by the N-APPJs, suggesting that acoustic signals may serve as a reliable proxy for estimating jet-induced force in future plasma diagnostics.

title:
Visible Femtosecond Soliton Molecules in a Mode-locked Pr
                    <sup>3+</sup>
                    -doped Fiber Laser
authors: Xiuyang Pang, Yuqiu Zhang, Jian Wu, Jinyong Leng, Pu Zhou
abstract: Soliton molecules have garnered significant attention owing to their rich dynamics and promising applications. While extensively studied in the infrared, their realization at visible wavelengths remains challenging, primarily due to the immature platform of visible ultrafast fiber lasers. Here, we demonstrate controllable multi-pulse dynamics and tightly bound femtosecond soliton molecules from a mode-locked Pr3+-doped fiber laser. The laser employs a linear cavity with nonlinear polarization rota tion for mode-locking and incorporates gratings for dispersion management. Stable single-pulse operation was achieved at 717.5 nm, yielding 164-fs pulses with a 5.07-nm bandwidth at a 50.276 MHz repetition rate. By increasing pump power, stable cavity bound multi-pulse states comprising 2 to 7 pulses were generated, featuring a minimum duration of 163 fs and a remarkably large temporal separation up to 1.7 ns. Furthermore, a hierarchical soliton molecule comprising 5 tightly bound pulse pairs with a 3.9 ps separation was further observed. This work establishes a new platform for generating controllable soliton molecules in the visible region, opening avenues for applications in underwater communication, bioimaging, and precision spectroscopy.

title: Green and Sustainable Computing: Efficient Strategies, Best Practices and Emerging Research Trends
authors: Murat Kilinc, Omer Faruk Ursavas, Fatih Gurcan, Ahmet Soylu
abstract: Green computing has become an increasingly significant research area due to the rising energy consumption and environmental impact associated with digital technologies. Although the number of publications has grown rapidly, the thematic fragmentation within the field makes it difficult to understand its overall structure and the evolution of research priorities over time. This highlights the need for a comprehensive and data-driven assessment of the green computing literature. The aim of this study is to map the thematic landscape of green computing and to analyze long-term research trends based on 11,119 journal articles published between 1960 and 2025. Using Scopus data and an embedding-based BERTopic methodology, semantically coherent topic clusters were identified. The findings emphasize two central insights. First, 36 meaningful topics were discovered, spanning areas such as energy-efficient virtualization, neuromorphic architectures, environmental modeling, and UAV-supported edge computation. Second, the trend analysis reveals a clear directional shift within the field. Traditional research areas focused on virtualization and hardware efficiency have declined, while distributed architectures, AI-driven systems, and environmentally aware solutions have emerged as dominant and rapidly growing themes. By providing an integrated overview of thematic structures and documenting major shifts in research priorities, this study offers a valuable foundation for future research and for guiding sustainable digital transformation strategies.

title: Joint evaluation of Lithium-ion Battery State of Health and Short Circuit by Relaxation Voltage
authors: Kun Yu, Peng Liu, Jiarui Zhang, Qinghua Gui, Lei Mao
abstract: Transportation electrification is an effective approach to reduce carbon emissions such that fuel-powered vehicles are gradually replaced by electrical vehicle (EV), which is powered by lithium-ion batteries mostly. With continuous usage of EV, battery performance decay can hardly be avoided. State-of-health (SOH) evaluation and internal short circuit (ISC) detection are crucial to ensure battery safety. However, battery degradation and ISC fault have similar impacts on battery voltage and temperature, making them difficult to be distinguished thus leading to possible safety risk. In this study, different effects of SOH and ISC on relaxation voltage of lithiumion battery are revealed and relaxation voltage-based SOH-ISC joint evaluation method is proposed. Relaxation voltage functions are inferred by equivalent circuit model (ECM), and two key function parameters are extracted and their relationships with SOH and ISC are established to realize joint evaluation. Through validation of accelerating aging test and ISC substitute experiments, the proposed method can provide accurate SOH estimation (error less than 2%) and distinguish different ISC levels (up to 200Ω short circuit resistance) simultaneously at wide lifespan (SOH from 80% to 100%). Furthermore, the proposed method can also keep such accuracy at high rate (up to 1.5C) charging-discharging scenery, which improves its applicability at practical application.

title: Conical Beam Conformal Holographic Leaky-Wave Antenna with Horizontal Polarization
authors: Somayeh Saeidi, Mohammad Amin Chaychi Zadeh, Ehsan Farokhipour, Andreas Rennings, Daniel Erni, Nader Komjani, Vahid Nayyeri
abstract: This paper introduces a single curvature conformal holographic leaky-wave antenna featuring a dominant TE mode. The radiation from the antenna takes on a conical beam and features horizontal polarization. This conformal leaky-wave antenna (CLWA) features an ungrounded Rogers RT6002 substrate with square patches printed on it in a nearly periodic arrangement wrapped on a cylinder. The absence of a ground plane leads to undesired radiation inside the cylindrical structure. We eliminated this undesired radiation by placing a PEC cylinder at a quarter guided operation wavelength away from the hologram. Also, the CLWA's launcher features six Vivaldi antennas arranged circumferentially around the cylindrical structure. The direction of the conical beam is considered to be 75° in relation to the axis of the cylindrical structure of the hologram. The simulated realized gain, radiation efficiency, and side lobe level (SLL) of at 12 GHz are about 10.9 dBi, 90%, and 7.5 dB, respectively.

title: TACIT: Trustworthy Arbitration through Calibrated Inference Tracking for rApp Conflict Resolution in O-RAN
authors: Joss Armstrong, Sheila Fallon, Enda Fallon
abstract: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) deployments face a coordination challenge when multiple radio applications (rApps) concurrently propose conflicting parameter changes.We present TACIT, a decentralized conflict resolution mechanism that advances the state of the art in three ways: (1) it resolves direct, indirect, and implicit conflicts without requiring explicit detection or classification, (2) it achieves O(N) computational complexity in the number of rApps, independent of parameter space size, and (3) it employs an asymmetric reputation mechanism that isolates unreliable rApps through continuous online learning. TACIT works by soliciting each rApp’s assessment of a proposed change’s impact on its own KPIs, then aggregating these self-interested inputs using reputation-weighted Nash Social Welfare optimization. No rApp needs to understand other rApps’ objectives, and no centralized function needs to replicate this understanding. Evaluation through ns-3 simulation with 19 cells and 7 concurrent rApps demonstrates that TACIT outperforms unconstrained operation, static priority ordering, and unweighted Nash Social Welfare across throughput, energy efficiency, and edge user metrics. When an rApp’s predictions degrade, the reputation mechanism isolates its influence within a single configuration window without operator intervention.

title: Active Learning Strategies for the Class Imbalance Problem
authors: Bokyung Amy Kwon, Kyungtae Kang
abstract: Recent advancements in data technology have greatly expanded data availability; however, extracting valuable information remains challenging due to high annotation costs and severe class imbalance. Active learning (AL) addresses this by minimizing annotation costs while maximizing model performance through selectively querying informative samples. While class imbalance often deteriorates model performance, it is prevalent in real-world scenarios. This study provides a comprehensive survey of the class-imbalance problem within active learning, analyzing regular articles published from 2018 to May 2025. We specifically posit that active learning can mitigate the imbalance problem as effectively as traditional approaches. Based on seminal literature, we propose a five-category taxonomy for imbalance mitigation: Data-level, Probability-based, Weight-adjustment, Loss-based, and Optimization approaches. Furthermore, we explore application-specific methods, evaluate key metrics, and highlight open challenges. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first topical review focusing on active learning for class-imbalance, offering valuable insights to the machine learning community.

title: A Unified Key Recovery Framework for Impossible Boomerang Attacks: Applications to Full-Round-ARADI and SKINNYe v2
authors: Lin Jiao, Xichao Hu, Dengguo Feng, Yongqiang Li, Senpeng Wang, Yonglin Hao, Xinxin Gong
abstract: The impossible boomerang attack is a powerful cryptanalytic technique, but existing key recovery methods face several limitations that restrict its applicability. Specifically, the key pre-guessing is coarse-grained, S-box details are ignored in the differential propagation, the complexity estimation and the key guessing order determination remain rudimentary. To overcome these issues, we introduce three key improvement measures. First, we propose a flexible partial key and difference pre-guessing technique based on directed graphs, enabling selective identification of required keys and differences for generating partial pairs and quartets. Second, we propose a pre-sieving technique to early eliminate invalid quartets by exploiting cipher-specific details. Third, we introduce an automatic key-guessing strategy based on the same directed graphs to efficiently determine valid guessing orders. We integrate these techniques to develop a unified key recovery framework for impossible boomerang attacks, accompanied by a formal and precise characterization of the overall complexity. This is the first framework to support flexible key and difference pre-guessing while incorporating block cipher details during key recovery for impossible boomerang attacks. Crucially, it enables the automatic generation of detailed recovery steps, a capability missing in prior work. As applications, under the four related-key/tweakey setting, we apply the framework to ARADI, a low-latency cipher proposed by the National Security Agency (NSA), and SKINNYe v2, a threshold-implementation-friendly cipher proposed at EUROCRYPT 2020. For ARADI, we achieve the first full-round attack with 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">130</sup> data, 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">253.78</sup> time, and 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">235.75</sup> memory complexity. For SKINNYe v2, we present the first 34-round impossible boomerang attack with 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">66</sup> data, 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">253.75</sup> time, and 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">239.75</sup> memory complexity. These results demonstrate the framework’s significance and its substantial improvement in advancing the impossible boomerang attack.

title: A 55-75 GHz Rectifier with 27% RF-to-DC Conversion Efficiency at 0 dBm in 22-nm FDSOI CMOS Technology
authors: Xinyu Kong, Ahmet Çağrı Ulusoy
abstract: This paper presents a high-efficiency millimeter wave (mmW) rectifier for wireless power transfer (WPT) systems, targeting enhanced performance at lower input power levels. Leveraging a differential cross-coupled topology in 22 nm FDSOI CMOS technology, the proposed rectifier achieves a peak power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 27% at 60 GHz with 0 dBm input power and maintains a PCE above 25% across a broad input power range from -2.5 to 5 dBm. It also demonstrates wideband operation, maintaining a peak PCE above 20% from 56 GHz to 69 GHz. This performance provides greater flexibility for transmitter design and enables longer WPT distances.

title: Co-Design of Energy Storage and Resource Management for Cell-Free Radio Access Networks
authors: Xiaojing Chen, Yijun Ding, Xiaomei Zhang, Wei Ni, Yichuang Sun, Xin Wang, Shunqing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Cell-free radio access network (CF-RAN) has emerged to address escalating demands in future mobile communications. The growing energy expense of CF-RAN necessitates efficient resource management and the integration of renewable energy harvesting systems. While prior studies on renewable energy-enabled CF-RAN have mainly focused on antenna resource management and energy consumption of access points (APs), they have largely overlooked significant power usage and scheduling requirements of central processing unit (CPU) and joint photovoltaic (PV) panel/battery sizing. To bridge these gaps, this paper integrates CF-RAN with smart grid technology and explores the joint optimization of PV panel/battery sizing and resource allocation to minimize system cost, including both capital expenditure (CAPEX) for PV/battery installation and operational expenditure (OPEX) for long-term operations. Leveraging the stochastic dual-subgradient method, we develop an online energy trading and resource allocation (OER) algorithm to enable real-time decisions on beamforming, service scheduling, and energy management. The feasibility and asymptotic optimality of the proposed OER are rigorously established through Lyapunov optimization techniques. Building on this asymptotically optimal solution, particle swarm optimization (PSO) is further employed to optimize the sizes of PV panels and batteries beforehand. Simulation results demonstrate a 62.7% reduction in total system cost compared to benchmark approaches, while reducing computational complexity in sizing PV panels and batteries.

title: Joint Temperature and Load Impacts on Hungarian Electricity Prices: Evidence From a Mamdani Fuzzy Inference System
authors: Ákos Botos, József Fogarasi, Márta Takács
publish: 2026 IEEE 13th International Joint Conference on Cybernetics and Computational Cybernetics, Cyber-Medical Systems (ICCC)
abstract: Electricity prices are driven by a complex interplay of demand and climatic factors, notably the system load and ambient temperature. Capturing these nonlinear, seasonal dynamics under conditions of climate variability poses a significant modeling challenge. In this study, we employ a Mamdani-type fuzzy inference system to simulate the joint effects of daily electricity system load and average temperature on wholesale electricity prices. This fuzzy logic approach provides a qualitative, rule-based mapping from inputs to price outcomes, enabling interpretability in the presence of uncertainty. Using historical data from 20162020 in Hungary, we define semantic categories (e.g. Low, Medium, High) for both load and temperature and construct intuitive IF-THEN rules reflecting expert knowledge of how extreme demand and weather conditions influence price levels. The fuzzy model is used as a scenario simulation tool rather than a precise predictor, illustrating how combinations of cold weather and high demand or hot weather and peak load lead to significantly elevated price regimes. The results indicate that the fuzzy inference surface aligns with economic intuition: extreme temperatures coupled with high system load yield high electricity prices, whereas moderate conditions result in lower prices. This demonstrates the usefulness of fuzzy inference in elucidating the joint temperature-load-price relationship under climate variability in an interpretable manner.

title: 300GHz Up- and Down- Converters Based on High-Power Schottky Diode Mixers for Wireless Links
authors: Veronica Lain-Rubio, Giacomo Ulisse, Jose Luis Gonzalez-Jimenez, Ignacio Barrueto, Javier Martinez-Gil, Oleg Cojocari
abstract: The demand for high-data-rate wireless connectivity is driving the development of next-generation communication systems operating in the D-band (110–170 GHz) and sub-THz range (220–330 GHz). In this work, we present a high-power, Schottky-diode-based sub-harmonic mixer integrated into a 220–330 GHz up- and down-converter (CUD) architecture. Experimental results demonstrate more than 10dB enhanced transmitted output power and low conversion loss (CL), enabling high-speed data transmission. We validate the CUD in a 300 GHz wireless link, achieving up to 42 Gbps throughput with a 64QAM modulation in 4 channels. These results highlight the potential of Schottky-diode-based CUDs for future high-frequency wireless networks.

title: Spatial-Temporal Dynamic Features Fusion Graph-Transformer Network for Traffic Flow Prediction
authors: Adnan A. Qaseem, Kai Sheng, Dejene M. Sime, Eshetie B. Atanaw
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: Accurate traffic flow prediction is essential for enhancing the efficiency and reliability of ITS. However, achieving high accuracy remains challenging due to the intricate spatial dependencies among road segments and the evolving temporal dynamics of traffic patterns across multiple time scales. This paper proposes a Spatial-Temporal Dynamic Feature Fusion Graph Transformer Network (STDF-GT) for traffic prediction. STDF-GT introduces a spatial-temporal feature extraction block that combines a Spatial Graph Convolution Network (Spatial Graph-Conv) and a Spatial Transformer Network (SpaFormer) to model both static and dynamic spatial correlations. A Gated Temporal Transformer Network (GTempFormer) is developed to capture short- and long-range temporal dependencies across multiple time scales. Additionally, a feature fusion layer with an attention mechanism effectively integrates the extracted spatiotemporal correlations. Experiments on four real-world traffic datasets show that STDF-GT outperforms baseline methods regarding prediction performance.

title: Causality-Powered Deep Learning Models for Stock Price Forecasting
authors: Linxuan Ren, Qian Cheng, Ru Hou
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: Accurately forecasting stock prices remains a central challenge in finance. In this study we integrate causality with deep learning techniques to provide explainable and reliable stock price forecasting. First, the convergent cross mapping algorithm is applied to quantify the causal dynamics between pairs of stock prices. Causal information is then embedded into various deep learning models to format the corresponding forecasting strategies. Extensive experiments are conducted on real financial datasets of Chinese stock markets and the corresponding numerical evidence suggests that embedding causality can effectively decrease the forecast errors and provide important insights for causal relations hidden in financial data.

title: Adversarial Domain Alignment Improves Root Biomass Prediction from SkySat Imagery_supp1-3693043.pdf
authors: Vasit Sagan
abstract: Root biomass plays a critical role in plant water uptake, nutrient acquisition, and terrestrial carbon cycling. However, estimating root biomass from satellite imagery remains challenging because roots are belowground and cannot be directly observed. Furthermore, inter-annual climate variability alters canopy spectral responses, causing domain shifts that degrade machine learning model generalization across growing seasons. In this study, we propose a domain-adaptive framework that aligns multi-year remote sensing features using a Domain-Adversarial Neural Network (DANN), followed by regression modeling on the aligned latent features. Using 1,355 soybean plots and 160 spectral, textural, and tasseled-cap features derived from multi-temporal SkySat imagery, baseline pooled performance yielded R² = 0.02. After DANN-based alignment, R² improved to 0.79–0.81 (ΔR² ≈ +0.77), with RMSE reduced from ~0.20 to ~0.08–0.09 kg plot⁻¹ (60% reduction in RMSE). Diagnostic experiments confirm that strict cross-year generalization remains limited without target-year information. These results demonstrate that domain alignment is essential for robust multi-year root biomass estimation using remote sensing.

title: An Event-Driven Dual-Mode 1280×1024 Cryogenic Infrared Vision Sensor for Adaptive Energy-Efficient Motion Perception
authors: Mingzhong He, Yixuan Du, Dezhen Yang, Yi Zhuo, Yufei Ai, Xiaoming Wang, Qingjiang Xia, Yacong Zhang, Zhongjian Chen, Songlin Yu, Wengao Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents an event-driven, dual-mode cryogenic infrared vision sensor that features a two-step time-encoding (TE) analog-to-digital converter (ADC) and a high-speed frame difference (FD) for motion detection. To enable always-on scene-adaptive motion perception with power optimization, the vision sensor is reconfigurable, supporting two operation modes: 1) Precise Imaging (PIM) and 2) Motion Detection (MD). In PIM mode, a two-step TE ADC is proposed for small-pixel-pitch infrared focal plane arrays (IRFPAs). The coarse conversion utilizes pulse frequency modulation (PFM) with an area-efficient true single-phase clock (TSPC) counter. This PFM employs a non-periodic-reset architecture to enable multi-subframe fusion and fine conversion of time-to-first spike (TFS), thereby improving the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) within a limited pixel area. In MD mode, a high-speed FD-based 1.5-bit event detection is implemented, using subframe coarse conversion without incurring additional memory cost or frame-rate loss. Compared with PIM mode, MD mode reduces power consumption by approximately 40% by deactivating fine conversion and partial output channels. A <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1280\times 1024$</tex-math> </inline-formula> prototype with a 15-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m pixel pitch was fabricated using a stacking process, in which the digital readout integrated circuit (DROIC) was implemented in a 0.18-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m CMOS process and interconnected with the HgCdTe IRFPA via indium bumps. In PIM mode, the prototype achieves 83.2dB SNR with a power consumption of 115.4 mW at 30.5 frames per second (fps) (1.49 pJ<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot \mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/step). In MD mode, with an 8-bit coarse conversion for event detection, the sensor consumes 58.83 mW at 976.6 fps (3.5 fJ/pixel-event), achieving the best event FoM among reported event-driven infrared vision sensors.

title: Bridging Legacy Smart Meters to Modern IoT Networks based on LoRaWAN
authors: Menno J. Faber, Helder R. O. Rocha, Wesley S. Costa, Higor A. F. Camporez, Marcelo E. V. Segatto, Jair A. L. Silva
abstract: The integration of legacy energy meters into modern smart grids remains a challenge due to the unviable cost and logistical complexity of full-scale replacement. In Brazil, this problem is acute, with millions of meters operating on a sort of Protocol for Advanced Measurement Infrastructure (AMI) legacy called PIMA (Protocolo para Infraestrutura de Medição Avançada), provoking incompatibility with modern IoT networks and impeding the rollout of AMIs. This letter presents a novel low-power sensor interface platform designed to resolve physical-layer data acquisition barriers in legacy infrastructure. The system architecture addresses the specific sensing constraints of open-collector serial outputs, decoding proprietary PIMA frames with high precision and transmitting key measurements over Long Range Wide Area Networks. An experimental validation confirmed the sensing accuracy, with a perfect match between transmitted data and the meter's physical display. This retrofit solution provides a verified and viable pathway for the rapid, scalable, and economical integration of legacy assets into modern AMI, bypassing the need for costly meter replacement.

title: Real-time Image-guided Radiotherapy via Joint Target Localization and Volumetric Reconstruction
authors: Jiashu Dong, Yanxin Wang, Yihang Zhang, Jiabing Xiang, Wenwen Zhang, Suqing Tian, Wei Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Radiation therapy is a critical treatment modality for malignant tumors, aiming to deliver a precise, spatially conformal radiation dose to the tumor while sparing the organs-at-risk (OARs). However, due to respiratory, gastrointestinal and urinary activity, internal patient anatomy undergoes motion which can compromise the accuracy of radiotherapy. To address this challenge, we propose a patient-specific deep learning (DL) framework capable of localizing tumors and OARs on X-ray digital radiographs (DRs) and simultaneously reconstructing volumes from single-view DRs in real-time. This framework first constructs a motion model based on the patient’s 4DCT image sequence, incorporating both global deformation and inter-organ interactions to accurately capture respiratory-induced anatomical variations. Next, LocatorNet, a rapid X-ray localization network, is pre-trained to efficiently determine spatial positioning. Guided by LocatorNet, CrossReconFormer is introduced, which integrates a high-performance 2D/3D cross-modal fusion module. This model effectively reconstructs high-fidelity CT images from single-view X-ray projections, preserving structural details and anatomical consistency. During treatment, real-time X-ray DRs are fed into the model, which yields both target localization and reconstructed CT images. Rigorous experiments conducted on patient pancreas and lung data achieve high accuracy and promising results. Clinically relevant dose calculation based on the reconstructed images indicates that our method holds significant clinical potential for accurate dose delivery, advancing the integration of DL in radiotherapy for enhanced patient care and treatment outcomes.

title: Risk-Aware Genetic Algorithms for Reliable Triathlon Relays: A Cantelli-Regularized Applied Mathematics Pipeline
authors: Attila Biró, Levente Kovács, László Szilágyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 13th International Joint Conference on Cybernetics and Computational Cybernetics, Cyber-Medical Systems (ICCC)
abstract: We present an applied mathematics pipeline for robust triathlon relay team selection across Sprint, Olympic, Half-Ironman, and Ironman distances. The approach couples a stochastic performance model with a Cantelli-regularized genetic algorithm to minimize expected relay time while controlling downside risk. Athlete–leg compatibility is guided by bioinformatics—inspired features-position weight matrix (PWM) motifs and hidden Markov model (HMM) pacing stability—providing interpretable selection pressure. Monte Carlo simulation evaluates mean, variance, and tail behavior, including conditional value-at-risk (CVaR). On historical event data, the method outperforms greedy and baseline GA approaches in both expected time and excess tail risk. The pipeline is modular, reproducible, and suitable for practice-led education in stochastic modeling, robust optimization, and data-driven decision support.

title: Integrated Scheduling Problem for Hybrid Job Shops Considering Dynamic Allocation of Multi-Skilled Workers
authors: He Mingfeng, Zhang Feng, Li Yifan, Qin Hongbin
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: This paper investigates an integrated dynamic scheduling problem in hybrid job shops, motivated by the operational characteristics of TFT-LCD module manufacturing, where automated and manual workstations operate in parallel and workers possess multiple skills that enable flexible cross-station assignment. A dynamic scheduling framework is proposed that adjusts the processing speed of manual operations through real-time workforce allocation and facilitates the dynamic deployment of multi-skilled workers across stations. A collaborative scheduling strategy integrating dynamic staffing and worker mobility is developed to enhance equipment utilization and balance workload distribution. A multi-objective optimization model is formulated to minimize both the makespan and the total number of workers. To solve this combinatorial optimization problem, a hybrid improved Artificial Bee Colony-Genetic Algorithm (HIABC-GA) is proposed. The algorithm employs a two-layer encoding scheme with real-time decoding to coordinate operation sequencing, dynamic workforce configuration, and personnel assignment. Global exploration is enhanced through genetic operators, while multiple neighborhood search strategies are incorporated to strengthen local exploitation and ensure search balance. Computational experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach and demonstrate its superiority over several existing methods for complex dynamic hybrid job-shop scheduling problems.

title: Research on Commercial Vehicle Segment Market Model Based on Spatiotemporal Continuity
authors: Nannan Xue, Shenglong Cheng, Yunshuang Zheng
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: The traditional market segment method for commercial vehicles mainly relies on static information such as vehicle model parameters and questionnaire surveys, which have problems such as low accuracy, limited coverage, and poor timeliness. In response to this situation, this article proposes a commercial vehicle segment market calculation method based on spatiotemporal continuity. This method utilizes vehicle networking trajectory data to extract effective parking points for vehicles through parking point recognition, merging of parking points, and removal of interference points; Subsequently, based on POI information, the docking points are matched with rules and weighted to obtain their segment market attributes; On this basis, a segment market correlation matrix and a transition matrix are constructed to model the segment market attributes of vehicle parking points from two dimensions: business relevance and temporality. The correlation matrix is used to characterize the business relationships between different segment markets, while the transition matrix reflects the dynamic transition patterns of segment markets in time series; The final output is the segment market category of the vehicle over a continuous period of time. The experimental results show that this method significantly outperforms traditional methods in terms of accuracy and timeliness in identifying segment markets, providing strong support for enterprises in market insights, product planning, and digital transformation.

title: Learning-Based Anomaly-Tolerant Resource Management in Cloud-Edge Continuum
authors: Hongyun Liu, Nils Barring
publish: 2026 IEEE 13th International Joint Conference on Cybernetics and Computational Cybernetics, Cyber-Medical Systems (ICCC)
abstract: In this work, we introduce two learningbased resource management approaches, DeepRM_ECO and DeepRM_AD, which extend Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based scheduling to explicitly incorporate anomaly detection and treatment. Both models are designed to minimize job slowdown while proactively identifying workloads that exhibit anomalous execution characteristics. DeepRM_AD integrates anomaly-aware decisions directly into the scheduling process, whereas DeepRM_ECO leverages heterogeneous resource capacities across edge and cloud nodes to implicitly separate anomalous workloads. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that both approaches substantially outperform the baseline DeepRM scheduler. Specifically, DeepRM_ECO and DeepRM_AD achieve mean job slowdowns of 5.4 and 3.2 respectively, compared to 9.8 for DeepRM. Moreover, DeepRM_ECO consistently attains high anomaly detection recall, ranging from 0.952 to 0.998 across different anomaly rates. These findings highlight the effectiveness of DRL as a unified mechanism for anomaly-tolerant scheduling and provide new insights into robust resource management in the cloud-edge continuum.

title: UAV-LDFNET: A Lightweight and High-Precision Model for Small Object Detection in Aerial Images
authors: Shihao Hu, Zhiwei Jia
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: This paper presents UAV-LDFNet, an algorithm designed to address the imbalance between lightweight design and detection accuracy in aerial small object detection. To achieve this, the model incorporates a Multi-expert Feature Extraction Module (MFEM), which utilizes parallel specialized convolutional branches to adaptively capture diverse spatial features and fine-grained details from complex backgrounds. Furthermore, we propose a Large Receptive Field Hybrid-scale Feature Pyramid (LRHS FPN) that employs expanded kernels and cross-scale skip connections to enhance global contextual modeling and optimize the fusion of multi-resolution features. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves lower computational overhead and higher detection accuracy, specifically increasing the mean average precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P 5 0}$</tex> by 2.1%. These results validate the superiority and efficiency of the proposed approach for real-time aerial scenarios.

title: NeuroMoE++: Patient-Adaptive Multi-Level Multimodal Fusion With Mixture-of-Experts for Neurological Disorder Classification
authors: Wajih Hassan Raza, Yu Wen, Aamir Bader Shah, Yidan Shen, Juan Martinez-Lemus, Mya Caryn Schiess, Timothy Michael Ellmore, Renjie Hu, Xin Fu
abstract: Early diagnosis of neurological disorders (NDs) benefits from multiple complementary signals, including anatomical MRI, diffusion imaging, functional activation, and clinical or serum biomarkers. These modalities capture distinct aspects of neurodegeneration and have motivated recent multimodal learning approaches. However, most existing methods combine modalities only at the feature or decision level through late fusion, meaning that modalities are processed independently and never influence one another during feature extraction. As a result, subtle cross-modality patterns that are highly informative in prodromal stages remain underutilized. To address this limitation, we propose NeuroMoE++, a hierarchical end-to-end framework that enables explicit interaction across modalities while preserving their individual structure. After obtaining unimodal and cross-modality predictions, a subject-driven adaptive integration produces patient-specific decisions. This combination allows the model to capture complementary structure, leverage fused evidence when beneficial, and remain interpretable from a clinical standpoint. Experiments on real clinical datasets demonstrate that NeuroMoE++ substantially outperforms unimodal and late-fusion baselines, achieving 84.94% accuracy. These results highlight the value of explicit cross-modality reasoning for practical neurological diagnosis.

title: : Efficient Privacy-Preserving Data Aggregation against Disclosure Attacks for IoTs_supp1-3672527.pdf
authors: zuyan wang
abstract: The growing popular awareness of personal privacy raises the following quandary: what is the new paradigm for collecting and protecting the data produced by ever-increasing sensor devices. Most previous studies on co-design of data aggregation and privacy preservation assume that a trusted fusion center adheres to privacy regimes. Very recent work has taken steps towards relaxing the assumption by allowing data contributors to locally perturb their own data. Although these solutions withhold some data content to mitigate privacy risks, they have been shown to offer insufficient protection against disclosure attacks. Aiming at providing a more rigorous data safeguard for the Internet of Things (IoTs), this article initiates the study of privacy-preserving data aggregation. We propose a novel paradigm (called <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RASE</monospace>), which can be generalized into a 3-step sequential procedure – noise addition, followed by random permutation, and then parameter estimation. Specially, we design a differentially private randomizer, which carefully guides data contributors to obfuscate the truth. Then, a shuffler is employed to receive the noisy data from all data contributors. After that, it breaks the correct linkage between senders and receivers by applying a random permutation. The estimation phase involves using inaccurate data to calculate an approximate aggregate value. Extensive simulations are provided to explore the privacy-utility landscape of our <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RASE</monospace>.

title: Health state estimation for complex systems with uncertain measurements based on Wiener process and belief rule base
authors: Hongdong Fan, Yu Zhao, Zhaoqiang Wang, Yu Yao, Jinying Li
abstract: In this paper, a new health state estimation method based on Wiener process and belief rule base (BRB) is proposed to evaluate the health state of a complex system with uncertain measurements caused by sensor failure or abnormal data transmission. First, the degradation process of each system component is modelled as a Wiener process, while the arrival of each observation is described by a Bernoulli-distributed binary random variable. Next, a state-space model with uncertain measurements is constructed, and its unknown parameters are estimated using the expectation–maximization (EM) algorithm. The calibrated degradation model can then be used to predict future degradation for each component. After that, to estimate the overall system health state, a BRB model is developed to fuse expert knowledge with the predicted degradation values of each component. Finally, a case study is presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in addressing health state prediction with uncertain measurements.

title: A Capacitor-Assisted MPPT Buck-Boost Interface for Low-Internal-Resistance Energy Harvesting
authors: Hongyuan Lu, Xinjie Yu, Jun Xu, Pengcheng Zhang, Songheng Yang, Zhe Xu, Longtao Yang
abstract: While buck-boost energy harvesting interfaces effectively decouple source and load, their ability to match low internal-resistance sources is inherently limited by the duty cycle constraint, which imposes a lower bound on the achievable input impedance. To overcome this limitation, this letter pro poses a six-switch dual-mode buck-boost interface featuring a capacitor-assisted maximum power point tracking (CA-MPPT) methodology. In the CA-MPPT mode, an auxiliary capacitor pre-biases the inductor current prior to the source connection, thereby introducing an additional degree of freedom to extend the achievable input impedance matching range toward low-internal resistance sources. Furthermore, a hybrid control algorithm is implemented to dynamically switch between conventional and CA-MPPT modes, minimizing auxiliary operation losses. Experimental results from a PCB prototype demonstrate a 30% reduction in normalized resistance mismatch, together with up to 7.4% higher input power and 13.5% higher output power.
