title: PVLM: Parsing-Aware Vision-Language Model With Dynamic Contrastive Learning for Zero-Shot Deepfake Attribution
authors: Yaning Zhang, Jiahe Zhang, Chunjie Ma, Weili Guan, Tian Gan, Zan Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The challenge of tracing the source attribution of forged faces has gained significant attention due to the rapid advancement of generative models. However, existing deepfake attribution (DFA) works primarily focus on the interaction among various domains in vision modality, and other modalities such as texts and face parsing are not fully explored. Besides, they tend to fail to assess the generalization performance of deepfake attributors to unseen advanced generators like diffusion in a fine-grained manner. In this paper, we propose a novel parsing-aware vision language model with dynamic contrastive learning (PVLM) method for zero-shot deepfake attribution (ZS-DFA), which facilitates effective and fine-grained traceability to unseen advanced generators. Specifically, we conduct a novel and fine-grained ZS-DFA benchmark to evaluate the attribution performance of deepfake attributors to unseen advanced generators like diffusion. Besides, we propose an innovative PVLM attributor based on the vision-language model to capture general and diverse attribution features. We are motivated by the observation that the preservation of source face attributes in facial images generated by GAN and diffusion models varies significantly. We propose to employ the inherent facial attributes preservation differences to capture face parsing-aware forgery representations. Therefore, we devise a novel parsing encoder to focus on global face attribute embeddings, enabling parsing-guided DFA representation learning via dynamic vision-parsing matching. Additionally, we present a novel deepfake attribution contrastive center loss to pull relevant generators closer and push irrelevant ones away, which can be introduced into DFA models to enhance traceability. Experimental results show that our model exceeds the state-of-the-art on the ZS-DFA benchmark via various protocol evaluations. Codes will be available at GitHub.

title: RadarSim : Toward an Intelligent Digital Twin for AI-Driven Radar Detection Systems
authors: Jinhao Wan, Chendong Jin, Chenyang Song, Taotao Li, Jie Su, Zhen Hong, Yiming Wu, Zhenyu Wen
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming radar detection from passive sensing to adaptive perception. However, developing robust AI radar algorithms is hindered by limited access to realistic and controllable testing environments. RadarSim introduces an intelligent digital twin framework that integrates scenario modeling, hardware-in-the-loop simulation, and closed-loop robustness evaluation. Built on a digital–analog–digital workflow, RadarSim enables LLM-based scene generation, dynamic orchestration of heterogeneous software-defined radios(SDRs), and unified testing under physical and adversarial perturbations. A distributed radar case study demonstrates its scalability and fidelity. By linking virtual modeling and physical emulation, RadarSim transforms radar validation from static experimentation into continuous intelligence evolution, offering a secure simulation foundation for future cognitive radar systems.

title: Fast and Efficient 6-DoF Grasp Estimation With Segment Anything Model in Cluttered Scenes
authors: Di-Hua Zhai, Sheng Yu, Yuanqing Xia
abstract: The task of executing object grasping in unstructured and cluttered environments is a significant challenge. Despite the development of various 6-DoF grasping methods to tackle this issue, rapid grasping objects from arbitrary viewpoints remains difficult. In this article, we introduce a zero-shot 6-DoF grasp pose estimation method for unstructured cluttered scenes, named FS-Grasp. Initially, we leverage the zero-shot capabilities of the segment anything model to perform object segmentation in cluttered scenes, thereby obtaining point clouds of unknown objects. Next, we design a zero-shot 6-DoF grasp pose prediction algorithm based on these object point clouds, enabling the detection of grasp poses for unknown objects in cluttered environments. In FS-Grasp, we introduce a multiscale, multiangle graspable region search algorithm that integrates transformers to conduct a comprehensive search for graspable poses. We conduct grasping tests across various datasets, and our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FS-Grasp can be effectively applied to most zero-shot grasping tasks. Furthermore, we apply FS-Grasp in diverse human–robot interaction scenarios, establishing an autonomous robot grasping framework based on visual language large models, which successfully performs the grasping and placement of multiple unknown objects, showcasing considerable practical application value.

title: Addressing Missing-Modality Data Quality Issues in Multimodal Remote Sensing Via Automated Encoder Architecture Search
authors: Xingsi Xue, Syed Muhammad Waqas, Mohammed J.F. Alenazi, Saru Kumari, Chien-Ming Chen, Nan Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing has become an essential paradigm for land-cover classification by exploiting complementary information from heterogeneous sensors. However, in practical applications, one or more modalities are often unavailable at inference time due to sensor failure, acquisition constraints, or environmental conditions. Such missing-modality scenarios introduce a systematic form of data quality degradation, which fundamentally undermines the reliability of conventional multimodal models and limits the effectiveness of manually designed compensation strategies. To address this challenge, this paper proposes PE-MMRSS, an automated encoder architecture design framework based on evolutionary neural architecture search for multimodal remote sensing classification under missing-modality conditions. Instead of relying on handcrafted hallucination architectures, PE-MMRSS constructs a unified and generic search space that jointly accommodates encoders associated with both available and missing modalities. A dual-part encoding scheme is designed to represent coupled teacher-student architectures, enabling effective evolutionary optimization. To further improve search efficiency, a lightweight LSTM-based Structural Compatibility Predictor is introduced to estimate the relative effectiveness of candidate architectures using early-stop validation signals, thereby significantly reducing the computational cost of architecture evaluation. Experimental evaluations across a diverse set of multimodal remote sensing benchmarks (Huston2013 and Augsburg) show that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared with the manually designed baselines in a wide range of missing-modality scenarios. PE-MMRSS achieves improvements of up to 0.5%-1.6% in overall accuracy and Kappa coefficient compared with the strongest handcrafted baseline, while reducing the architecture search cost to approximately 1.4 GPU-days. These results indicate that automated neural architecture search provides an effective and efficient solution for mitigating data quality degradation in multimodal remote sensing systems.

title: The untapped potential of computer modeling for solving challenges in microwave electronics
authors: Gennadiy Churyumov, Aleksandr Gritsunov, Evgeniy Odarenko, Eduard Khutoryan, Ihor Kuzmychov
abstract: The paper reviews current methods for simulating complex physical processes in vacuum electron devices. For research aimed at gaining the most insight into the nonlinear beam-wave interaction mechanism, we used numerical-analytical mathematical models based on a self-consistent set of motion equations (describing charge-particle motion), the Poisson equation (describing the space-charge field), and the excitation equation (describing changes in RF field amplitudes). The development stages of these models and a detailed design scenario for microwave devices are outlined. To verify the accuracy of this scenario, we focused research and design efforts on several microwave devices, particularly a magnetron gun with a cold cathode and a two-stage magnetron. The computer modeling results closely align with the experimental data.

title: GTFA-Net: A Generative Network for High-Resolution Time-Frequency Analysis in Dual-Frequency Continuous Wave Radar Tracking
authors: Yipeng Ding, Minhao Ding, Zhixiang Zhang, Runjin Liu, Bowen Tang
abstract: Dual-frequency continuous wave (DFCW) radar is a promising technology for indoor target tracking, yet its performance is constrained by the low time-frequency resolution (TFR) and frequency ambiguity of traditional time-frequency analysis (TFA) methods, such as the short-time Fourier transform. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a novel semi-supervised network named GTFA-Net, which integrates a non-local mechanism for enhanced TFR. The proposed GTFA-Net integrates a Various Noise Maker (VNM), a Multi-Scale Short-Time Module (MDSTM), and an Energy Concentration Module (ECM) with a non-local mechanism. The proposed VNM enhances the robustness of the network by adding different types of noise to the input signal, while the MDSTM improves the richness of time-frequency information by introducing multiple short-time filtering windows. The ECM, incorporating an enhanced non-local attention mechanism, effectively extracts global time-frequency features. Experimental results demonstrate that GTFA-Net significantly outperforms traditional TFA methods in terms of noise immunity, energy concentration, and instantaneous frequency extraction accuracy, making it a promising solution for high-precision indoor target tracking.

title: I Have A Claw! Impact of Embodying A Biomimetic Avatar on Self-perception and Task Performance in Virtual Reality
authors: Titouan Lefrou, Théo Lefeuvre, Florian Dufresne, Charlotte Dubosc, Olivier Christmann, Geoffrey Gorisse
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Embodied representations are known to influence user behavior in virtual environments through stereotypes associated with their appearance, a phenomenon known as the Proteus effect, or through perceived affordances. The latter concept refers to the suggested ways of interacting with the environment as perceived by the capabilities of the user's virtual representation. The inherent ability of virtual reality to support non-anthropomorphic avatars, enabling new interaction modalities, paves the way for a deeper understanding of affordances in virtual environments. We designed an experiment to assess the effect of embodying a chimeric hand (crab claw), compared to a regular hand, on participants' sense of embodiment, presence, character plausibility, mental workload, and behavior. Following a within-subject design, participants took part in a gamified task consisting of finding a key in a wrecked pirate ship. As the key could be hidden in containers of different durability, their strategy (opening or destroying objects) was used as an objective measure. Results indicated a high perceived usability and a low mental workload with both virtual limbs. Participants also performed hitting actions more frequently when using the biomimetic limb than when using the humanoid hand representation. A higher perceived change in the body schema was reported when embodying the crab claw, but a higher sense of body ownership and agency were observed with the anthropomorphic hand. Results revealed no significant difference in terms of plausibility.

title: Sparse Tableau-Based Dynamic Simulation for Active Distribution Feeders
authors: Anjali Mandokhot, Vassilis Kekatos, Dionysios Aliprantis
publish: 2026 IEEE Power and Energy Conference at Illinois (PECI)
abstract: This paper presents an open-source sparse tableau-based simulation platform specifically designed for modeling and analysis of unbalanced active distribution feeders. Network components are modeled using differential-algebraic equations in abc variables, capturing unbalanced conditions due to single- or multi-phase laterals and unbalanced loads. The sparse tableau approach simultaneously solves for all network variables, enabling direct variable access. The platform also provides a high-fidelity representation of inverter-based resources with comprehensive control modeling capabilities. A layered architecture enables independent incorporation of new component models and analysis methods, making this simulation platform a versatile research tool for inverter-dominated distribution systems.

title: A Realistic Control Approach for Set Stabilization of Complex Biological Networks With Logical Models_supp1-3692800.docx
authors: Kwang-Hyun Cho
abstract: Driving cellular biological systems to one of desired long-term behaviors, an important subject in systems biology, can be interpreted as the set stabilization problem. This article presents a realistic control approach to set stabilization of complex biological networks modeled by Boolean networks (BNs) wherein only open-loop control is applied. First, the considered BN is transformed into an auxiliary BN having redundant state variables. Based on the similarity between desired attractors and Boolean algebra, a number of reduced BNs are constructed from the auxiliary BN in favor of set stabilization. Next, the feedback vertex set (FVS) control law is applied to reduced BNs for obtaining open-loop control inputs that take partial state variables toward desired values. This procedure continues in a recursive way until ensuring that the controlled BN converges to an unspecified desired attractor. The proposed scheme has the superiority that both fixed-point and cyclic attractors can be included in the problem setting, and that it can tackle complex BNs with moderate computational burdens. Extensive numerical experiments on random BNs and real biological systems are provided to demonstrate the applicability of the proposed scheme.

title: Design of Intelligent AED Monitoring System for Key Public Places
authors: Siqi Wu, Yangfan He, Xiaoyun Qi, Yuwen Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Asia Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Machine Learning (CACML)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases have become a prominent public health challenge in China, the critical golden rescue window for cardiac arrest lasts only 4-6 minutes. As a core intervention for cardiac arrest, automated external defibrillators (AEDs) are a key support for establishing an efficient emergency response system. An integrated solution incorporating advanced intelligent technologies such as facial recognition and WeChat miniprograms is proposed, aiming to develop an intelligent AED management system with two core objectives: First, achieving real-time AED response through precise device positioning, dynamic path planning, and facial recognition unlocking technology, significantly reducing rescue preparation time and enhancing emergency response efficiency; Second, establishing a digital supervision framework covering the entire lifecycle of devices, standardizing key processes such as AED maintenance, periodic calibration, and consumable replacement, while providing intelligent support for maintenance personnel. Practical application results show that the entire process from user contact with the device to completing facial recognition and unpacking takes approximately 10 seconds, and the complete process from device recovery to box sealing requires only 20 seconds. With physical auxiliary lighting, facial recognition accuracy can reach 90%. This system revolutionizes the traditional use and management of automated external defibrillators (AEDs) through digital means, offering a practical and innovative solution to improve the success rate of cardiac arrest resuscitation.

title: Enhancing Security and Integrity of Public Kiosk Systems Against Privilege Escalation
authors: Jean Rosemond Dora, Ladislav Hluchy, Friday Ikechukwu Agu
publish: 2026 IEEE 13th International Joint Conference on Cybernetics and Computational Cybernetics, Cyber-Medical Systems (ICCC)
abstract: Public Access Kiosk Systems (PAKS) pose a significant security challenge due to the sensitive resources they often manage and their public accessibility. One of the most common threats to these systems is unauthorized “privilege escalation (PE)”, which allows malicious users to defeat specific security mechanisms and grant themselves higher privilege (to gain more control) over the underlying operating system (OS). This is a technique commonly used by attackers or penetration testers in real-life attack scenarios. This paper introduces the MultiLayered Security Enforcement (MLySE) model, a novel defense framework designed to enhance the security and integrity of PAKS by actively reducing the likelihood of PE attacks. The MLySE architecture combines a defined Policy-Based Access Control (PBAC) framework with a dynamic Run-time Integrity Monitoring (RIM) environment. In an experimental study against a suite of 10 known PE exploits, the MLySE model achieved an average 92.4% reduction in Attack Success Rate (ASR) compared to conventional baseline security implementations, while maintaining an insignificant CPU overhead of roughly 3.4%. The results demonstrate that MLySE offers a resilient, feasible security paradigm for public-facing Kiosk environments (KEs).

title: Dependence of optical refractive index of seawater on temperature, salinity, pressure, and wavelength in near-infrared waveband for warm and shallow sea
authors: Funa Zhuang, Jing Wang, Bohai Zhang, Bo Yin, Shanshan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Dependence of optical refractive index (RI) of seawater on temperature, salinity, and pressure is crucial for optical fiber sensors operated in seawater. Expressions of RI of seawater on temperature, salinity, pressure, and wavelength applicable to near-infrared waveband are developed based on a great deal of RI data measured by a semi-encapsulated microfiber Mach-Zehnder interferometer sensor. To verify the accuracy of these expressions, RI measured by infrared refractometer, theoretical calculated sensitivity and experimental measured sensitivity are used, by which the most accurate expression is determined. Based on this expression, a variable sensitivity matrix is established to demodulate temperature, salinity and pressure simultaneously with errors of 10.91 %, 6.09 % and 18.80 %, respectively. The expression proposed here will provide valuable references for theoretical and experimental study on optical fiber sensors operated in infrared waveband and push the applications of fiber optical sensors in ocean investigations.

title: Toward Wireless Human-Machine Collaboration in the 6G Era
authors: Gaoyang Pang, Wanchun Liu, Chentao Yue, Daniel E. Quevedo, Karl H. Johansson, Branka Vucetic, Yonghui Li
abstract: The next industrial revolution, Industry 5.0, will be driven by advanced technologies that foster human-machine collaboration (HMC). It will leverage human creativity, judgment, and dexterity with the machine's strength, precision, and speed to improve productivity, quality of life, and sustainability. Wireless communications, empowered by the emerging capabilities of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks, will play a central role in enabling flexible, scalable, and low-cost deployment of geographically distributed HMC systems. In this article, we first introduce the generic architecture and key components of wireless HMC (WHMC). We then present the network topologies of WHMC and highlight impactful applications across various industry sectors. Driven by the prospective applications, we elaborate on new performance metrics that researchers and practitioners may consider during the exploration and implementation of WHMC and discuss new design methodologies. We then summarize the communication requirements and review promising state-of-the-art technologies that can support WHMC. Finally, we present a proof-of-concept case study and identify several open challenges.

title: From Generation to Detection: Multigenerator, Multitype, and Multirole LLM-Generated Vaccine Misinformation
authors: Syed Talal Ahmad, Haohui Lu, Sidong Liu, Annie Lau, Amin Beheshti, Mark Dras, Usman Naseem
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have significantly improved text generation capabilities. However, they also present challenges, particularly in generating vaccine-related misinformation, which poses risks to public health. Despite research on human-authored misinformation, a notable gap remains in understanding how LLMs contribute to vaccine misinformation and how best to detect it. Existing benchmarks often overlook vaccine-specific misinformation and the diverse roles of misinformation spreaders. This article introduces VaxGuard (<uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/usmaann/VaxGuard</uri>), a novel dataset designed to address these challenges. VaxGuard includes vaccine-related misinformation generated by multiple LLMs and provides a comprehensive framework for detecting misinformation across various roles. Our findings show that GPT-3.5 and GPT-4o consistently outperform other LLMs in detecting misinformation, especially when dealing with subtle or emotionally charged narratives. On the other hand, PHI3 and Mistral show lower performance, struggling with precision and recall in fear-driven contexts. Additionally, detection performance tends to decline as input text length increases, indicating the need for improved methods to handle larger content. These results highlight the importance of role-specific detection strategies and suggest that VaxGuard can serve as a key resource for improving the detection of LLM-generated vaccine misinformation.

title: 8-Channel Silicon Photonics Transceiver Arrays for 896 Gb/s PAM-4 Transmission Over 2 km
authors: Chenyang Zhong, Yujun Xie, Xingyu Wang, Peng Wang, Ming Li
abstract: High-speed and scalable optical interconnects are increasingly required in modern data center infrastructures driven by bandwidth-intensive computing workloads. Here, we demonstrate an 8-channel silicon photonics (SiPh) transmitter based on Mach-Zehnder modulators (MZM) and an 8-channel receiver based on germanium-silicon photodetectors (PD). The measured 3-dB bandwidths reach 47 GHz for the MZMs and 35 GHz for the PDs, with good channel-to-channel uniformity. Furthermore, using a 2 km single-mode fiber to connect the MZM and PD devices, 112 Gb/s PAM-4 transmission per channel is successfully demonstrated, corresponding to a total data rate of 896 Gb/s. This demonstration highlights the scalability of SiPh device arrays and supports their application in high-capacity data center optical interconnects.

title: Pre-processing Pipeline and Imaging Equipment Impact the Performance of CNN for Breast Cancer Detection in Mammograms: Evidence from CBIS-DDSM and VinDr-Mammo
authors: Nickerson Ferreira, André Peres, César Rennó-Costa, Renan C. Moioli
abstract: As the leading cause of cancer-related death among women worldwide, breast cancer mortality can be significantly reduced by early detection of lesions by mammogram. The integration of artificial intelligence, particularly convolutional neural networks, can enhance the performance of computer-aided detection (CAD) systems, improving both diagnostic accuracy and early detection. However, building foundational models for real clinical settings is challenging due to the nonuniformity of data across social and clinical contexts, which can affect data quality, quantity, and distribution and, in turn, machine learning performance. This study investigates the effect of variations in mammography equipment and diverse pre-processing techniques on the performance of deep learning models for breast cancer detection in mammograms. First, we used public datasets comprising digitized analog (CBIS-DDSM) and digital (VinDr-Mammo) images to evaluate the effects of normalization, image resizing, and filtering on breast cancer classification performance. Then, we extend this analysis to different pre-processing pipelines and data from mammography manufacturers Siemens, Planmed, and IMS Giotto. Model performance varied significantly across mammography equipment manufacturers, with maximum ROC score values ranging from 0.91 for IMS Giotto (using ResNet18) to 0.62 for Siemens (using EfficientNet-B3). In addition, we show that classification performance across datasets is related to different pre-processing pipelines, with no single optimal approach. Together, our findings contribute to the ongoing quest for robust, generalizable machine learning models in settings with scarce and diverse public health data.

title: Robust and Accurate Magnetic Anomaly Detection under Complex Noise Using Synchrosqueezed Short-Time Fourier Transform and Attention-Based Residual Network
authors: Pengfei Zhang, Changlong Li, Ying Shen, Zhiyue Chen, Xuyang Guo, Yi Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Magnetic anomaly detection (MAD) is a vital passive sensing technique widely applied in underwater target detection, geological exploration, and military defense. A major challenge lies in accurately identifying weak signals in complex scenarios, particularly under low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) conditions. Traditional and machine learning-based methods suffer from limited robustness and reliance on hand-crafted features. Although deep learning approaches enhance feature extraction, they often struggle with insufficient time–frequency resolution and noise sensitivity. In this study, a novel MAD method integrating the synchrosqueezed short-time Fourier transform (SSFT) with a residual neural network (ResNet), enhanced by a convolutional block attention module (CBAM) simultaneously, is proposed. SSFT is used to convert 1D time series into the 2D time-frequency spectrum with a high energy concentration. While CBAM is used to adaptively emphasizes salient features. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a detection accuracy of 77.4% and 6.1 false alarms per kilometer at an SNR of –3 dB, outperforming conventional methods (OBF: 63.5%, ME: 50%) and learning-based models (SVM: 70.6%, 1D-CNN: 67.5%, WVD-CNN: 69.25%, BiLSTM: 59.85%). The effectiveness and robust superior of the proposed method, achieving a detection accuracy of 94% with the lowest false alarms per kilometer, is further validated by field survey scenarios.

title: A Wavenumber-Domain Imaging Algorithm for Terahertz Synthetic Aperture Radar with Variable-Curvature Trajectory
authors: Xu Wang, Tongxu Hu, Jiahao Zhang, Jialu Liu, Jin Li, Rui Min, Zongjie Cao, Yiming Pi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: During real flight missions, the terahertz synthetic aperture radar (THz-SAR) system often fails to follow a perfectly ideal path consistently. Non-ideal trajectories induce parameter mismatches in conventional imaging methods, resulting in the invalidation of imaging algorithms and thus posing a significant challenge to high-precision imaging. To address this challenge, this research proposes an imaging method in the wavenumber domain for THz-SAR with variable-curvature trajectory (VCT). Firstly, the rate of change of the radius of curvature (RCRC) is introduced to establish a variable-curvature model (VCM). Next, range resampling is employed to eliminate height errors in the radar data envelope, while a phase compensation function is constructed to remove height errors embedded in the phase, thereby equivalently projecting the 3-D spatial motion trajectory onto a 2-D planar curve. Subsequently, the RCRC is derived via the fitting of the 2-D planar curved trajectory, and the precise wavenumber spectrum is derived via the series inversion method, followed by the compensation of non-space-invariant phase errors. Finally, phase functions are respectively constructed along the scene area in the wavenumber-domain to compensate for residual phase errors, yielding high-precision imaging results. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm for THz-SAR imaging under non-ideal trajectory conditions is confirmed through both simulation and experimental results.

title: Fast Measurement for Lithium-Ion Battery Based on Phase-Optimized Frequency-Divided Hybrid Excitation Signal
authors: Mingqiang Lin, Keyu Dai, Jinhao Meng, Fengxiang Wang
abstract: Battery impedance serves as a critical source of information for state and fault analysis. However, accurate and efficient impedance measurement remains challenging, this paper introduces a collaborative scheme that integrates a frequency-divided hybrid excitation signal with a Hierarchical Bayesian Distribution of Relaxation Times (HB-DRT) inversion framework. The proposed scheme simultaneously achieves wide frequency coverage, high energy efficiency, and enhanced measurement accuracy. The hybrid excitation signal is formed by a weighted fusion of a Chirp signal and an optimized multisine signal, enabling rapid coverage of both low frequencies and medium-to-high frequencies. This combination effectively balances broad spectral measurement with a high signal-to-noise ratio at key frequencies. HB-DRT inversion framework operates without preset parameters and autonomously adjusts its hyperparameters to achieve effective noise suppression, ensuring high-precision reconstruction of impedance spectra under diverse conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method completes impedance measurement over the 0.1-2000 Hz range within 10 seconds. Under various state-of-charge and temperature conditions, the normalized root-mean-square error remains below 0.86%.

title: A Model Predictive Control Approach with Three-Dimensional Generalized Optimal Creep Model for Adhesion Control of High-speed Trains
authors: Deqing Huang, Ruiqi Li, Zhengang Zhang, Yu Long, Luanhui Li
abstract: Running high-speed trains (HSTs) at the high-adhesion point can significantly enhance energy efficiency and actively prevent wheel slip. However, due to the complex and variable nature of wheel–rail contact conditions, traditional optimal adhesion estimation methods based on the two-dimensional λ–μ model are difficult to be applied to all practical contact scenarios, often resulting in inaccurate or failed identification of the adhesion peak point. In this article, a model predictive control (MPC) approach combined with a three-dimensional generalized optimal creep model (3-D-GOCM) is proposed to achieve efficient adhesion utilization. The proposed 3-D-GOCM can identify the optimal slip ratio in real time under various wheel–rail conditions. First, a single-axle wheelset dynamic model with a virtual control variable is established. Second, the 3-D-GOCM is developed using exponential fitting, which actually extends the well-established O. Polach model. Finally, an adhesion controller based on MPC theory is designed to track the optimal slip ratio and ensure good control performance. The simulation results in StarSim hardware-in-the-loop platform verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Sensorless Control of CPMSM via Hybrid Reaching Law-Based ISMO and LCO-Assisted SOGI
authors: Hanning Qian, Zhaolong Sun, Zhentian Liu, Fengrui Cui, Junhe Zhang, Wei Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The conical permanent magnet synchronous motor (CPMSM) offers superior efficiency in shaftless propulsion systems due to its unique topology. However, structural constraints preclude the installation of mechanical sensors. Consequently, the control performance relies heavily on the precision of sensor-less observers. Furthermore, precise estimation is severely challenged by time-varying parameters induced by axial displacement and aperiodic load pulsations. To address these issues, this paper first quantifies the impact of axial displacement on CPMSM parameters. Based on this, a composite sensorless control strategy is proposed. This strategy integrates a hybrid reaching law-based integral sliding mode observer (HRL-ISMO) with a limit cycle oscillator (LCO)-assisted second-order generalized integrator (SOGI). Firstly, an integral sliding surface is introduced to suppress the high-frequency chattering inherent in traditional observers. Subsequently, a hybrid reaching law combining variable exponents and terminal attractors is designed. This law accelerates system convergence while further enhancing chattering suppression. On this basis, an LCO is employed to stabilize the fundamental amplitude. Combined with a SOGI, this strategy significantly enhances the back-EMF waveform quality. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits strong robustness and excellent dynamic response under complex operating conditions. It provides a reliable solution for applying CPMSMs in shaftless propulsion systems.

title: High-efficiency High-isolation Dual-frequency Magnetron using Cascaded Axial Extraction with Intended Impedance Mismatching
authors: Young-Wook Kim, Dong-Hyun Oh, Jung-Hoon Han
abstract: While relativistic magnetrons offer significant advantages such as high output power, high efficiency, and compactness, they are limited by their narrow-band characteristics. To address this issue, we propose a dual-frequency system designed by cascading two magnetrons to extract RF power through a single circular output waveguide. Specifically, to ensure high-efficiency operation, an intended impedance mismatching structure and end-caps are employed to minimize interference and transmission loss between the two magnetrons. The proposed system utilizes a cascaded all cavity axial extraction (CACAE) structure to simultaneously extract power from both magnetrons and radiate it in the TM01 mode. The target operating frequencies of the magnetrons are 4.8 GHz and 5.8 GHz. Simulation results using CST Particle Studio (CST-PS) confirm that under an operating voltage of 125 kV and a magnetic field of 0.35 T, the total system operates with an efficiency of 59%, achieving peak output power of 344 MW. The interference frequency isolation levels (IFIL) for the two magnetrons were confirmed to be -23.77 dB and -27.23 dB, respectively.

title: Optimization-Based Behavioral Modeling of Mixers for Frequency Comb OFDM Radar Processing
authors: Umut Utku Erdem, Henning Poensgen, Taewon Jeong, Lucas Giroto, Benjamin Nuss, İbrahim Kağan Aksoyak, Ahmet Cagri Ulusoy, Thomas Zwick
abstract: This paper presents an optimization-based behavioral model for mixers driven by multi-tone local oscillator (LO) signals, considered specifically for frequency comb orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) radar applications. Unlike traditional models, the proposed approach is designed and tested for multi-tone LO excitations. The model uses polynomial nonlinearities for both intermediate frequency (IF) and LO ports, supported by spectrum-domain fitting that selectively emphasizes strong intermodulation products. In addition, a polynomial block is introduced to capture input power-dependent phase nonlinearity. The approach is validated using circuit-level simulations and supported by measurements. Radar processing results show the model replicates distortive effects in simulations. The proposed model enables rapid system-level performance estimations and waveform optimization, replacing computationally expensive circuit-level simulations.

title: Metaverse Framework for Wireless Systems Management
authors: Ilias Chrysovergis, Alexandros-Apostolos A. Boulogeorgos, Theodoros A. Tsiftsis, Dusit Niyato
abstract: This article introduces a metaverse framework designed for simulation, emulation, and interaction with wireless systems. The framework integrates extended reality (XR), digital twins (DTs), artificial intelligence (AI), the internet of things (IoT), blockchain, and advanced 5G/6G networking solutions to create a platform for both system development and management. Using XR, users can interact with complex systems, while DTs enable real-time monitoring and optimization. AI improves system performance, and IoT devices provide real-time sensor data to increase simulation accuracy. In addition, blockchain ensures secure, decentralized interactions, and wireless networks offer the infrastructure for uninterrupted communication. This framework serves as a tool for exploring, developing, and optimizing wireless systems, with the aim of offering a significant understanding into the future of networked environments. The framework achieves sub-10 ms decision-to-action latency through integrated operation of all six layers, demonstrated via a UAV positioning use case that delivers <1 ms URLLC connectivity, sub-10 ms AI inference, 12 ms ray-tracing updates, and 3–6 s blockchain finality with audit trails, while the RL-optimized positioning policy significantly improves network coverage for disadvantaged users.

title: Contextual Semantic Relevance Predicting Human Visual Attention_supp1-3683873.pdf
authors: Kun Sun

title: GaN based PAM Inverter performance compared with Silicon based PWM VSI motor drive
authors: Debjani Chakraborty, Vedant Chendake
abstract: In inverter-based motor drives, the phase current ripple is one of the key drivers of motor loss and thus temperature rise. This current ripple is inversely proportional to the pulse width modulation (PWM) switching frequency. By increasing switching frequency, the phase current ripple reduces and therefore Gallium nitride (GaN) technology is useful in efficient high switching operation. Consequently, electrolytic capacitors are eliminated leading to improved system power density and reliability. Field oriented control (FOC) helps minimize current and torque ripple. However, FOC with GaN at high current bandwidth control increases computational load on the MCU, cost of design and EMI at motor terminals due to high switching transients. In case of some medium (>80V) and high voltage (HV) applications, high switching transients at motor terminals leads to higher motor EMI, bearing current loss and degradation. Therefore, in this paper a study is shown comparing the performance of a GaN based pulse amplitude modulation (PAM) inverter trapezoidal motor drive with the Silicon (Si) based FOC voltage source inverter (VSI) drive. The PAM is comprised of a GaN based synchronous buck converter switched at 500kHz. Output of PAM is connected to a silicon-based inverter which drives the motor in 12-step/ 150-degree block commutation at motor's fundamental frequency (speed) at full duty-cycle without electrolytic capacitors. The Si VSI FOC solution is switched at 20kHz with 400uF electrolytic DC bus capacitance. The results show that even with PAM inverter motor drive using block commutation, the light load efficiency is higher than FOC solution, also the MCU utilization is reduced, and full load performance is very close to FOC. Thus, GaN PAM using 12-step trapezoidal algorithm offers advantages of GaN technology compared to Si VSI FOC, with significantly reduced computation effort for high or ultra-high speed motor drive.

title: Research on Vehicle Agents for Emergency Evacuation under Marine Storm Surge Scenarios
authors: Jiahan Ma, Hongda Zhou, Yuanjin Li, Weihong Li
abstract: Increasing global extreme weather events make marine storm surges a critical threat to coastal urban transportation and evacuation efficiency. However, existing research faces challenges in dynamically coupling road capacity with evacuation demand, particularly lacking vehicle agent designs that synthesize disaster perception, decision-making, and interaction. To address this, this paper proposes a vehicle agent model that integrates a multi-source perception strategy, a three-layer decision-making architecture combining Large Language Model (LLM) semantic understanding with the D* Lite algorithm, and refined vehicle behavior simulation. Multiple sets of controlled experiments were designed on a simulation platform, which simulates real-world disaster effects such as flooding and visibility reduction, to investigate the impact of various factors on vehicle evacuation under storm surge scenarios, verifying the effectiveness of the designed vehicle agent model in emergency evacuation scenarios during storm surges. Rigorous statistical evaluations based on 30 repeated simulation runs demonstrate that: (1) extreme environmental factors, namely severe water depth and low visibility, compound to trigger micro-level vehicle stalls and collisions, critically degrading macroscopic road capacity; (2) surging traffic demand exacerbates local deadlocks in traditional models; and (3) the proposed three-layers architecture dynamically mitigates congestion propagation, shortening the average evacuation time by 24.2% compared to traditional rule-based models. Ablation results further confirm that the proposed model effectively suppresses performance variance, ensuring a highly robust and collision-reduced evacuation under unpredictable storm surge conditions.These findings offer significant theoretical and practical contributions to emergency management, enhancing the resilience of urban transportation systems against storm surge disasters.

title: Circularly Polarized Array Antenna for Polarimetric SAR Application
authors: Mohamed Gaagai, Giulia Buttazzoni, Abdelheq Boukarkar, Massimiliano Comisso, Fateh Benmahmoud, Osama Mahfoudia, Azzedine Bouaraba
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this letter, the design, simulation and measurement of a novel circularly polarized array antenna for polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR) applications in the C-band are presented. A low-cost and compact solution is achieved by employing microstrip technology. More precisely, a multilayer configuration is adopted, where the sequential rotation technique, combined with the use of slotted parasitic elements in a stacked arrangement, ensures wideband operation and high polarization purity. The proposed array antenna exhibits compact dimensions (62 mm × 62mm × 13mm) with low weight of only 56 g. Measurements performed on a fabricated prototype show that the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|S_{11}|$</tex-math></inline-formula> parameter remains below -10 dB from 4.82 GHz to 7.62 GHz (corresponding to a 45% bandwidth), with the axial ratio below 3 dB from 4.95 GHz to 6.85 GHz (corresponding to a 32% bandwidth), which is fully encompassed by the impedance bandwidth. Moreover, the measured far-field patterns in the anechoic chamber are in excellent agreement with the simulated results. Hence, the proposed array antenna represents a promising candidate for PolSAR systems, where challenging radiation requirements coexist with stringent compactness constraints.

title: Generative Consistency Models for Estimation of Kinetic Parametric Image Posteriors in Total-Body PET
authors: Yun Zhao, Qinlin Gu, Georgios I. Angelis, Andrew J. Reader, Yanan Fan, Steven R. Meikle
abstract: Dynamic total body positron emission tomography (TB-PET) makes it feasible to measure the kinetics of the tracer in all organs of the body simultaneously which may lead to important applications in multi-organ disease and systems physiology. Since whole-body kinetics are highly heterogeneous with variable signal-to-noise ratios, parametric images should ideally comprise not only point estimates but also measures of posterior statistical uncertainty. However, standard Bayesian techniques, such as Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), are computationally prohibitive at the total body scale. We introduce a generative consistency model (CM) that generates samples from the posterior distributions of the kinetic model parameters given measured time-activity curves and arterial input function. CM is able to collapse the hundreds of iterations required by standard diffusion models into just 3 denoising steps. The CM was evaluated using physiologically realistic simulations and an application to a subject’s dynamic [18F]FDG TB-PET dataset analyzed with a standard single-input two-tissue compartment model. When trained on 500,000 physiologically realistic two-tissue compartment model simulations, the CM produces similar accuracy to MCMC (median absolute percent error < 5%; median K-L divergence < 0.5) but is more than five orders of magnitude faster. CM produces more reliable Ki images than the Patlak method by avoiding the assumption of irreversibility, while also offering valuable information on statistical uncertainty of parameter estimates and the underlying model. The proposed framework removes the computational barrier to routine, fully Bayesian parametric imaging in TB-PET and is readily extensible to other tracers and compartment models.
